title: Threaded connector
abstract: The object of the invention is a mechanical connector assembly for joining pipes conveying fluids such as hydrocarbons. The connector joins two internally threaded pins (7a) and (7b) attached to pipe sections (3) to an externally threaded box (1). Alternatively, a single pin (7) anchored to a pipe section may be anchored to a box (8) joined to the opposite pipe section (3). Redundant mechanical liquid seals provide a wedge seal (12) as well as a thrust seal (6) where an extension of the pin (7a) abuts against the other pin (7b) or where it bears against the box (8). The connection is made by applying external pre-stress to the pin, allowing the box (1) to be rotated with limited resistance before releasing the external pre-stress, resulting in a high level of pre-stress sufficient that the thrust shoulders (5) of the pin (1) will not separate before the applied load approaches the yield strength of the connector material. This provides a double liquid seal consisting of a thrust shoulder (6) in addition to a wedge seal (12) which may confine viscous material that further enhances extrusion pressure at the termination of the threads (2a and 2b). Alternatively, the joint consisting of a single pin (7) and box (8) may be pre-stressed, providing a sliding bearing allowing the two components to be rotated independently. By pre-stressing the threads, stress ranges are significantly reduced and provide very high fatigue capacity. Finally, ultimate strength of the connector both in tension and in compression will exceed that of the pipe section.
claims:
Claims A method for providing a mechanical connection for joining two pipe sections having end components thereof referred to as pins which have threads of opposite hands and are anchored into a matching oppositely threaded sleeve referred to as a box, the pins each having an elongated section between their threads and free end, wherein the connection is pre-stressed with hydraulically or otherwise powered jacks to a force maintained by providing a predetermined length of elastically compressed elongated sections between the threads of the pins, and by engaging the oppositely threaded sleeve in the threads of the pins, as well as maintaining a full, or significant part of the thickness of the elongated pin sections in compressive force up to the tensile strength of the pipe sections, wherein the total length of the elastically compressed elongated pin sections may range from twice the thickness of the pipe sections to twenty pipe section thicknesses and the elongated pin section thickness may range from half the thickness to twice that of the pipe section, whereupon, after installation, the pre- stressing devices are released such that a significant part of the pre- stressing force remains as tensile force in the box and compressive force in the pins, whereby, when tensile load is applied to the connection, the compression in the pins will maintain a thrust seal at the free ends of the pins. A method for providing a mechanical connection for joining two pipe sections, one section being attached to an outwardly threaded end component referred to as a pin and the other section being attached to an internally threaded end component referred to as a box, the pin and box having matching threads threaded together, wherein the pin has an elongated section between its threads and free end and the box having an internal receptacle for the elongated section of the pin extending from its threads to an abutment for the free end of the elongated section of the pin, the connection being pre-stressed with hydraulically or otherwise powered jacks, to a force maintained by providing a predetermined length of elastically compressed elongated pin section between the threads of the pin and the abutment of the 

 box, and by rotating the sections of pipe with respect to each other to tighten the matching threads, as well as maintaining a full, or significant part of the thickness of the elongated pipe section in compressive stress up to the point where the elongated pipe section abuts against the abutment in the box, wherein the length of the elastically compressed elongated section of the pin may range from three times the pipe section thicknesses to twenty pipe section thicknesses, and the thickness of the elongated pin section may range from half the thickness to twice that of the pipe section, whereupon, after installation, the pre-stressing devices are released, such that a significant part of the pre-stressing force is maintained as tensile force in the box and compressive force in the pin, whereby, when tensile load is applied to the connection, the compression in the pins will maintain a thrust seal between the free end of the pin and the abutment in the box. A method according to claim 1 , wherein wedge seals at the free ends of the elongated pin sections or at other locations are used with an additional thrust seal where the elongated pin sections abut against each other in load conditions. A method according to claim 3, wherein two small ribs are supporting each wedge seal, which preferably confine a viscous substance such as thread lubricant in order to increase extrusion pressure. A mechanical connection for joining two pipe sections having end components thereof referred to as pins which have threads of opposite hands and are anchored into a matching threaded sleeve referred to as a box, the pins having sealing surfaces in abutting relationship at their free end, wherein the connection is pre-stressed in a predetermined length of elastically compressed elongated pin section material between the threads of the pins, the pre-stressing being maintained by the matching threaded sleeve in engagement with the threads of the pins. 6. A mechanical connection according to claim 5, wherein the total length of the elastically compressed elongated pin sections between the threaded parts of the pins ranges from three to twenty pipe section thicknesses, and wherein preferably the thickness of the pre- stressed predetermined length ranges from half to twice the thickness of the pipe section. 7. A mechanical connection for joining two pipe sections, one of the sections having an end component referred to as a pin and comprising external threads that are anchored into internal threads of a matching threaded sleeve referred to as a box and being an end component of the other pipe section, the pin having a sealing surface at its free end thereof in engagement with a sealing surface inside the box, wherein the connection is pre-stressed in a predetermined length of elastically stressed parts extending between the threads and the sealing surfaces, wherein the predetermined length ranges from three to twenty pipe section thicknesses, wherein the thickness of the compressed parts preferably ranges from half the thickness to twice that of the pipe section, and wherein compressive force in the pin maintains a thrust seal between the sealing surfaces when tensile load is applied to the connection. 8. A mechanical connection according to claim 6 or claim 7, wherein wedge seals at the ends of the threaded pin sections or at other locations are present with an additional thrust seal where the pipe sections abut against each other in complete contact at all supported load conditions. 9. A mechanical connection according to claim 8, wherein two small ribs are supporting each wedge seal, which preferably confine a viscous substance such as thread lubricant in order to increase extrusion pressure. 10. A mechanical connection according to any one of claims 5 - 9, wherein the end components each have at least one collar for engagement of hydraulically or otherwise powered jacks for applying pre-tensioning force to the end components. 11. A method according to claim 1, wherein the total length of the elastically compressed elongated pin sections ranges from 4 to 14 pipe section thicknesses, preferably from 6 to 10, more preferably from 5 to 7 pipe section thicknesses. 12. A method according to claim 2, wherein the length of the elastically compressed elongated pin section ranges from 4 to 14 pipe section thicknesses, preferably from 5 to 10, more preferably from 6 to 8 pipe section thicknesses. 13. A mechanical connection according to claim 5, wherein the predetermined length of elastically compressed elongated pin material ranges from to 20, preferably 6 to 12, more preferably 5 to 8 thicknesses of the pipe sections. 14. A mechanical connection according to claim 7, wherein said predetermined length ranged from 3 to 20, preferably from 4 to 14, more preferably from 5 to 10, most preferably from 6 to 8 times the wall thickness of the pipe sections.
